title: Questions Re Leliels Insides and Leliel
display_title: Questions Re Leliels Insides and Leliel
wikitext:
[]: Questions Re: Leliel's Insides and Leliel             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Thu Nov 13, 2008 12:35 am Okay, search function is acting up, so admins, lock this if there is already a thread about this. I just have a few questions about Leliel that have been nagging me for quite some time now... 1. What is exactly inside Leliel? Is it honestly a different space? And if so, do the laws of physics apply to that dimension? 2. How the hell did Shogouki find her way out into Leliel's shadow when she fell into Leliel herself?! Relatively easier... 3. Is Leliel's shadow 2-dimensional, i.e., if one drives around its shadow he or she would see the same pattern as the other side (the front pattern travels around with the viewer) ? Or is it actually 3-dimensional, and I should blame GAINAX for making it look funny? When Leliel disappeared, I believed that I saw a very flat circle disappear, not a sphere disappear. Discusso.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Thu Nov 13, 2008 12:41 am We know that there is some kind of hyper-dimensional nature to this angel, and not just because we are told so by Ritsuko; the fact that the angel could teleport and apparently move faster than light, and the fact that it could apply the same effect to Unit 01, is evidence thereof.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Nov 13, 2008 12:59 am 1) According to Ritsuko, a Sea of Dirac. Physics? who knows. 2) Because, as Ritsuko says, they are connected. The way that I look at it is, Leliel is kind of like a 4 dimensional being (spacial dimensions) existing in 3 dimensional space. In the same way as if you took a hula hoop, and pass it through a 2 dimensional piece of paper. In 2 dimensions, the hoop looks like 2 circles, but they are really connected. Likewise, in 3 dimensions, Leliel looks like a thin blob and a ball, but they are really connected. 3) Leliel's shadow looks 3 dimensional to me, the patterns change and there's even spherical shading along its surface. It probably looks like a flat disc when it disappears because it becomes transparent first.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Nov 13, 2008 1:51 am Too bad they put nothing but the 'matrix loading room' inside... would have been an awesome place to add extreme creepiness. Event Horizon, anyone?

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Thu Nov 13, 2008 1:58 am Ornette wrote: if you took a hula hoop, and pass it through a 2 dimensional piece of paper.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Nov 13, 2008 5:55 am It's arbitrary what the object is. I used a hoop as an example because in a 2 dimensional slice, it looks like 2 separate things (2 circles). I used a piece of paper as an example of something that is 2 dimensional, a plane. You're not ripping through the paper anymore than Leliel is ripping through the 3 dimensional space that exists in Eva, it's a metaphor. Since I don't exist in 2 dimensional space, I have no idea what 2d existence would look like, but if suddenly a 2d world had a 3d object inside of it, like the hoop through the paper, only a part of it would exist in 2 dimensions (in the case of the hoop, 2 circles). I'm simply using that example to try to illustrate what a 4d object in 3d space would be like.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Nov 13, 2008 12:14 pm see also: mice. RIP Douglas Adams

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Nov 13, 2008 1:43 pm Okay, better example of this metaphor. Pretend you're a 2-D person in a 2-D world. Everything is flat for you, but God puts a Hula Hoop through your world. Not touching or damaging anything, it's just passing by. You see this. The end of the Hulahoop looks like a little oval at first, then it splits into two circles for a little while, then it becomes the oval again, then it's gone. But in reality, it was just one big plastic ring passing through.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Nov 13, 2008 9:55 pm 3454350 hours in MS paint

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Thu Nov 13, 2008 10:16 pm WAAAHHH I GET IT NOW! ONWARD TO STRIING THEORY!/offtopic kewlz thanx guys

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Fri Nov 14, 2008 10:59 am http://de.youtube.com/watch?v=JkxieS-6WuA A "simple" explanation of how to imagine multi dimensional world with their paradoxons. I think it should be really helpfull and interesting. I assume this theory explains the Evangelion falling into shadow and comming out of the "ball" out.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 14, 2008 2:08 pm I still want to know why they call the sphere a "shadow". It's clearly part of Leliel's physical body, no? An actual "shadow" is just an optical phenomenon. Wonder what the deal behind those white stripes is, too. They vanish when Leliel dies.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Nov 14, 2008 2:17 pm I still want to know why they call the sphere a "shadow". It's clearly part of Leliel's physical body, no? An actual "shadow" is just an optical phenomenon.

[]:             Quote Post by Silent Lightning » Fri Nov 14, 2008 2:55 pm They seem reminiscent of dazzle camouflage.

[]:             Quote Post by Recall » Fri Nov 14, 2008 4:02 pm Reichu wrote: I still want to know why they call the sphere a "shadow". It's clearly part of Leliel's physical body, no? An actual "shadow" is just an optical phenomenon. Wonder what the deal behind those white stripes is, too. They vanish when Leliel dies.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Fri Nov 14, 2008 4:16 pm Recall wrote: Its a fantasy film, why can't the shadow be the body? A shadow is the description as thats the words we would use to assoiciate with what we saw, what would you call it if it was infront of you? I really don't like how closed minded this place is with its thinking.

[]:             Quote Post by Recall » Fri Nov 14, 2008 4:25 pm Formless One wrote: Recall wrote: Its a fantasy film, why can't the shadow be the body? A shadow is the description as thats the words we would use to assoiciate with what we saw, what would you call it if it was infront of you? I really don't like how closed minded this place is with its thinking.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Fri Nov 14, 2008 5:15 pm Recall wrote: It still can be dark "particle wave matter" rather than a psychical form you can touch, i wasn't aware angels met certain criteria. Just because the angel before and the angel after had a pshyical shape as their body, doesn't mean that this one must. Wasn't their an angel that was technological virus based that got into the HQ? Yes it manifested itself into a physical form but the "virus" itself was the angel. Im bored.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 14, 2008 5:24 pm Recall wrote: A shadow is the description as thats the words we would use to assoiciate with what we saw, what would you call it if it was infront of you?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Nov 14, 2008 5:46 pm Asking questions must be the new "closed-minded" Also, this thread might help with a few things (Leliel in general): http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=1418
